hook-body.content:
A page builder is often pictured as “JSON in, React out”—one big component tree. I wanted something with clearer seams. So here it’s a **pipeline**: **Load** → **Expand** → **Resolve** → **Render**. Each step does one job. Most of it is pure and synchronous, so it stays testable and predictable.

The main idea: **separate structure from environment**. Structure—sections, blocks, trigger refs—is built in sync steps from JSON and definitions. Environment stuff (asset base URLs, CDN, 3D inlining) happens in a single **Resolve** step and one async boundary. By the time the client gets the page, everything is already resolved; no key or URL resolution on the client.

Below is the pipeline in practice: the orchestrator, then each stage, with the real code written out (this site isn’t open source, so the snippets live on the page).

hook-quote.text: Structure is built in sync steps from JSON. Environment-dependent work happens in one Resolve step and a single async boundary.
